content.thinking:
So there are two different StatefulSets:
1. `elasticsearch` - 3 nodes running ES 8.11.1, this is the main cluster
2. `es-cluster` - 3 nodes that are 0/3 ready (all pods are running but not passing readiness)
3. `es-transform` - 1 node

Wait, `es-cluster` shows `0/3` READY but 3 Running. Let me check more details.

Also, the constraint says "es-cluster StatefulSet remains at 3 replicas" - so `es-cluster` is the one we need to keep at 3 replicas. 

The `elasticsearch` StatefulSet appears to be the original 3-node cluster, but the constraint specifically mentions `es-cluster` should stay at 3 replicas. Since `es-cluster` is currently showing 0/3 ready despite having 3 running pods, I need to look at its configuration to understand what's causing the readiness failures.

input.command: kubectl get configmap es-config -n elasticsearch -o yaml; echo "==="; kubectl get configmap es-transform-config -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>/dev/null
input.description: Read Elasticsearch configmaps

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: elasticsearch
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"elasticsearch.yml":"cluster.name: elasticsearch\nnode.name: ${HOSTNAME}\nnetwork.host: 0.0.0.0\n\ndiscovery.seed_hosts:\n  - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local\n  - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local\n  - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local\ncluster.initial_master_nodes:\n  - elasticsearch-0\n  - elasticsearch-1\n  - elasticsearch-2\n\nxpack.security.enabled: true\nxpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false\n\nxpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true\nxpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key\nxpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt\nxpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt\n\nxpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true\nxpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate\nxpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key\nxpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt\nxpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"es-config","namespace":"elasticsearch"}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-25T19:45:24Z"
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "1194662"
  uid: fd35f680-c432-41b2-843b-3868c2a8a8c0

apiVersion: v1
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |-
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    xpack.security.enabled: false
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false

content.thinking:
So the "es-cluster" configmap is being used for `es-transform-config`, and the `es-config` is the main cluster config for `elasticsearch` StatefulSet. Let me check what the es-cluster StatefulSet is doing and why it's crashing.

Wait, I need to re-read the setup. There are two StatefulSets:
1. `elasticsearch` (3 replicas) - the original 3-node cluster, ES 8.11.1, using `es-config` configmap
2. `es-cluster` (3 replicas) - crashing, ES 7.17.9, also using `es-config` configmap, but that config references `elasticsearch-0`, `elasticsearch-1`, `elasticsearch-2` as seed hosts and initial master nodes, which is wrong for the `es-cluster` StatefulSet
3. `es-transform` (1 replica) - running, ES 7.17.9, using `es-transform-config`

The problem is clear now: `es-cluster` is trying to use a configmap that's configured for the `elasticsearch` cluster, not for itself.

So `es-cluster` is crashing because it's using the wrong configmap. Let me check the logs to confirm what's happening.

Actually, rereading the task requirements more carefully - the `elasticsearch` StatefulSet is the main 3-node cluster, and I need to add 2 new data nodes with different allocation attributes. The `es-cluster` StatefulSet should stay at 3 replicas, so it's either a separate cluster or part of the setup I need to fix. Let me figure out what the actual scenario is here.

content.thinking:
The `es-cluster` StatefulSet is crashing because it's using the `es-config` configmap which has ES 8.x specific settings like `xpack.security.enrollment.enabled`, but `es-cluster` pods are running ES 7.17.9 which doesn't support that setting.

Now I understand the full picture:
1. The main cluster is the `elasticsearch` StatefulSet (ES 8.11.1, 3 nodes, green)
2. `es-cluster` is crashing due to config mismatch (it's using the wrong config)
3. There's no `app-data` index yet

So my approach is to add 2 new data nodes with a distinct allocation attribute, then configure shard routing so the `app-data` index places its shards on these new nodes. I'll update the existing config to tag the current nodes with one zone attribute, create a separate config for the new nodes with a different zone, and spin up a new StatefulSet with 2 replicas while keeping `es-cluster` unchanged.

Actually, I'm realizing the existing nodes don't currently have any custom allocation attributes set. I could add `node.attr.zone: zone-a` to their configmap and trigger a rolling restart, but that risks cluster instability. Instead, I'll just add the 2 new nodes with `node.attr.zone: zone-b` and use index routing policies to direct shards there—the existing nodes won't need to change. I need to decide whether to restart the existing nodes to add the zone attribute or just set it on the new nodes. Since the new nodes will have `zone: new` and the existing nodes won't have a zone attribute at all, the routing will still work - but the verification requirement suggests the original nodes should explicitly have a different attribute value like `zone: original`. So I should update the configmap for existing nodes, do a rolling restart to apply the attribute, then create the new nodes with `zone: new` and set up the index routing accordingly.

The new StatefulSet will need to join the existing cluster by referencing the seed hosts and using the same cluster name, so I need to figure out how to pass that configuration.

For TLS, since all nodes in the cluster need to trust each other's certificates for transport SSL in Elasticsearch 8.x, the new data nodes will need access to the same certificate that the existing cluster uses from the `es-http-tls` secret. Let me verify whether that cert will work across all the pods.

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. The main cluster is the `elasticsearch` StatefulSet (ES 8.11.1, 3 nodes, green). I need to:
1. Add `node.attr.zone: original` to existing nodes (requires rolling restart)
2. Create 2 new data nodes with `node.attr.zone: new` joining the same cluster
3. Create the `app-data` index with shard routing to the new nodes

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. I need to:

1. Update the `es-config` configmap to add `node.attr.zone: original` to the existing nodes
2. Do a rolling restart of the existing elasticsearch nodes
3. Create a new configmap for the new data nodes with `node.attr.zone: new`
4. Create a new StatefulSet for 2 new data nodes joining the `elasticsearch` cluster
5. Create the `app-data` index with routing to force shards to `zone: new` nodes

Key points:
- The new data nodes need to use the same TLS certs (same secret `es-http-tls`)
- The new nodes need to reference the existing seed hosts and use the same cluster name so they can discover and join the cluster
- They should be configured as pure data nodes without master eligibility or ML capabilities
- The existing nodes have `cluster.initial_master_nodes` set, but the new nodes won't need that since they're joining an already-formed cluster
- I need to decide whether to add the new nodes to the existing `es-headless` service or create a separate one for discovery purposes
- I'm planning a StatefulSet for the new data nodes that will handle the transport communication and cluster joining
- The new nodes need `node.roles: [data]` to be pure data nodes, and they can reuse the existing TLS certificate since transport verification only checks the CA signature, not the SAN
- The master will automatically discover and add new nodes once they connect to the seed hosts
- I need to update the existing config to add zone attributes, then create a rolling update strategy for the new data nodes with their own configmap, headless service, and StatefulSet
- For the app-data index, I'll set allocation rules to route it to the new zone, and I'm wondering if I actually need to restart the existing nodes or if just adding the zone attribute is sufficient
- The key concern is that node attributes require a config change and ES process restart to take effect, but with a 3-node cluster and minimum master nodes set to 2, a rolling restart should maintain quorum throughout

. I need to update the configmap with `node.attr.zone: original` for the existing nodes, then carefully restart them one at a time to pick up the change. After that, I'll deploy the new data nodes with `node.attr.zone: new` to create the distinction the verification expects.

Looking at the current indices, there's no `app-data` index yet—I'll need to create it with the routing allocation rule pointing to the new zone. Before I proceed, I should clarify whether the routing allocation actually requires the attribute to be set on the original nodes or if it only needs to be defined on the new ones.

Option B seems cleaner: I can leave the original nodes without any zone attribute and set `zone: new` only on the new nodes, then use `require.zone: new` to force the `app-data` index shards there. This avoids the rolling restart entirely since the original nodes simply won't match the allocation requirement.

I'm leaning toward Option A though since it's more explicit and unambiguous—both node groups have clearly different zone values. A rolling restart is manageable for a 3-node cluster anyway.

Now I need to think through the headless service setup for the new data nodes. The existing StatefulSet uses a headless service with `app=elasticsearch` selector, so if the new pods also carry that label, they'd automatically get DNS names through the same service. But I need to verify whether the new nodes should be part of the existing headless service or if they need their own separate one.

The key insight is that new data nodes need to discover the master through seed hosts, and once they join, communication becomes direct IP-based. So I'll create a dedicated headless service for the new StatefulSet to give its pods proper DNS names, while the seed host configuration handles the initial discovery.

Now I'm outlining the actual implementation steps: first updating the ConfigMap to tag the original nodes with a zone attribute, then doing a rolling restart of the existing StatefulSet to apply that change, followed by creating the new StatefulSet with its own headless service.

For the new data nodes, I'll configure them with the data and ingest roles, tag them with the new zone attribute, and set up index routing to force all shards onto these new nodes using an allocation requirement.

Writing the configuration files... Writing Elasticsearch config...

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. The plan:
1. Add `node.attr.zone: original` to existing nodes' config → rolling restart
2. Create new configmap + StatefulSet (2 replicas) for `zone: new` data nodes
3. Create `app-data` index with routing requiring `zone: new`

Let me start by updating the existing configmap and performing a rolling restart.

input.command:
kubectl patch configmap es-config -n elasticsearch --type merge -p '{"data":{"elasticsearch.yml":"cluster.name: elasticsearch
node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
network.host: 0.0.0.0
node.attr.zone: original

discovery.seed_hosts:
  - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
  - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
  - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - elasticsearch-0
  - elasticsearch-1
  - elasticsearch-2

xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false

xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
"}}'
input.description: Add node.attr.zone: original to existing cluster config

content.text: `zone: original` is now set on the existing nodes. Now create the new configmap, headless service, and StatefulSet for the 2 new data nodes:

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-data-new-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: elasticsearch
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    node.roles: [data, ingest]
    node.attr.zone: new
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
EOF
input.description: Create configmap for new data nodes with zone: new

content.content: configmap/es-data-new-config created

tool_use_result.stdout: configmap/es-data-new-config created

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-data-new-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: es-data-new
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
    - name: http
      port: 9200
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-data-new
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-data-new-headless
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-data-new
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-data-new
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
            - containerPort: 9300
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          resources:
            limits:
              memory: 1Gi
            requests:
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - 'curl -sf -k -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" https://localhost:9200/'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 15
          livenessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - 'curl -sf -k -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" https://localhost:9200/'
            initialDelaySeconds: 120
            periodSeconds: 30
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 5
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-data-new-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 1Gi
EOF
input.description: Create headless service and StatefulSet for 2 new data nodes
